source.definition: to continue steadfastly despite difficulties or obstacles

explanation.sourceText: The English word "persevere" comes from the Latin "perseverare", which combines "per" meaning 'through' and "severare" meaning 'to persist or be strict', thus conveying the sense of continuing firmly or steadfastly.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „persevere“ provine din latinescul „perseverare“, care combină „per“ însemnând „prin“ și „severare“ însemnând „a persista sau a fi ferm“, exprimând astfel ideea de a continua cu hotărâre.

source.additional: Regular verb. Often used in contexts related to endurance or persistence to describe the ability to manage a difficult situation or challenge until the end.

target.definition: a nu ceda în ciuda dificultăților sau a presiunilor

explanation.sourceText: Derived from Latin "perseverare", composed of "per" (through) and "severare" (to persist), meaning 'to hold out, to persist'.
explanation.targetText: Provine din latinescul „perseverare“, care înseamnă a persista sau a rezista în fața dificultăților.

target.additional: Verb used to describe the ability to remain steadfast in difficult situations. It is often found in motivational contexts.

examples.sourceText: I persevere, no matter how hard it is.
examples.targetText: Eu perseverez, indiferent cât de greu este.

examples.sourceText: Do you persevere until the end?

examples.targetText: El/Ea persevereză în ciuda tuturor dificultăților.

examples.sourceText: Persevere, it will get better soon!
examples.targetText: Perseverați, va deveni mai bine în curând!
El persevera în ciuda tuturor obstacolelor.

examples.sourceText: I will persevere, no matter what happens.
examples.targetText: Voi persevera, indiferent ce se va întâmpla.

examples.sourceText: I would persevere if I had to.
examples.targetText: Aș persevera dacă ar fi necesar.

learningTips.text: The Romanian word "persevera" sounds very similar to the English "persevere", making it easy to remember as both mean to keep going despite challenges.
Think of a marathon runner who keeps running even when tired—that's what "a persevera" means: to keep pushing forward.
The prefix "per-" in both English and Romanian means 'through', so "persevera" literally means to 'carry through' difficulties.
Remember the phrase "persevere and prevail" to link the English and Romanian words and their meaning of enduring until success.
